metadata.description:
Django: Full-stack Web Frameworks: ORM/Admin/Templates. MTV Architecture. DRF for Building APIs. ### Explanation: - **Full-stack Web Frameworks**: These are frameworks that provide tools and libraries to handle both the client-side (front-end) and server-side (back-end) of web applications. - **ORM/Admin/Templates**: - **ORM (Object-Relational Mapping)**: A programming technique for converting data between incompatible type systems using object-oriented programming languages. In Python, Django's ORM is a good example. - **Admin**: Typically refers to an administrative interface that allows users to manage the application’s content and settings. For instance, in Django, you can use `admin.site.register` to register models with the admin site. - **Templates**: Used for rendering dynamic web pages. In Django, templates are used extensively. ### Example Python Code (Django ORM): ```python from django.db import models class Author(models.Model): name = models.CharField(max_length=100) def create_author(name): author = Author(name=name) author.save() return author # Usage author = create_author('John Doe') ``` ### Example Python Code (Django Admin): ```python from django.contrib import admin from .models import Author @admin.register(Author) class AuthorAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): list_display = ('name', 'id') # Register the model with the admin site. admin.site.register(Author, AuthorAdmin) ``` ### Example Python Code (Django Templates): ```html {% for author in authors %}

{{ author.name }}

{% endfor %} ``` ### MTV Architecture: - **M**odel: Represents the data and business logic of the application. - **T**emplate: Used to render dynamic web pages. - **V**iew: Handles requests, interacts with models, and selects templates. ### Example Python Code (Django View): ```python from django.shortcuts import render from .models import Author def author_list(request): authors = Author.objects.all() return render(request, 'author_list.html', {'authors': authors}) ``` ### DRF for Building APIs: - **DRF** stands for Django REST Framework. It is a powerful and flexible toolkit that makes building Web APIs in Python easy. ### Example Python Code (DRF API View): ```python from rest_framework import viewsets from .models import Author from .serializers import AuthorSerializer class AuthorViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet): queryset = Author.objects.all() serializer_class = AuthorSerializer ``` These examples provide a basic understanding of how these concepts can be implemented in Python using Django and DRF.

data_pointers.title: Django
data_pointers.content_preview:
Django: Full-stack Web Frameworks: ORM/Admin/Templates. MTV Architecture. DRF for Building APIs. ### Explanation: - **Full-stack Web Frameworks**: These are frameworks that provide tools and libraries to handle both the client-side (front-end) and server-side (back-end) of web applications. - **ORM/Admin/Templates**: - **ORM (Object-Relational Mapping)**: A programming technique for converting data between incompatible type systems using object-oriented programming languages. In Python, Django's ORM is a good example. - **Admin**: Typically refers to an administrative interface that allows users to manage the application’s content and settings. For instance, in Django, you can use `admin.site.register` to register models with the admin site. - **Templates**: Used for rendering dynamic web pages. In Django, templates are used extensively. ### Example Python Code (Django ORM): ```python from django.db import models class Author(models.Model): name = models.CharField(max_length=100) def create_author(name): author = Author(name=name) author.save() return author # Usage author = create_author('John Doe') ``` ### Example Python Code (Django Admin): ```python from django.contrib import admin from .models import Author @admin.register(Author) class AuthorAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): list_display = ('name', 'id') # Register the model with the admin site. admin.site.register(Author, AuthorAdmin) ``` ### Example Python Code (Django Templates): ```html {% for author in authors %}

{{ author.name }}

{% endfor %} ``` ### MTV Architecture: - **M**odel: Represents the data and business logic of the application. - **T**emplate: Used to render dynamic web pages. - **V**iew: Handles requests, interacts with models, and selects templates. ### Example Python Code (Django View): ```python from django.shortcuts import render from .models import Author def author_list(request): authors = Author.objects.all() return render(request, 'author_list.html', {'authors': authors}) ``` ### DRF for Building APIs: - **DRF** stands for Django REST Framework. It is a powerful and flexible toolkit that makes building Web APIs in Python easy. ### Example Python Code (DRF API View): ```python from rest_framework import viewsets from .models import Author from .serializers import AuthorSerializer class AuthorViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet): queryset = Author.objects.all() serializer_class = AuthorSerializer ``` These examples provide a basic understanding of how these concepts can be implemented in Python using Django and DRF.

content.full:
## 定义
Django 是一个基于 Python 的高级 Web 框架，遵循模型-视图-模板（MVT）架构模式，旨在快速开发安全、可维护的网站。

## 何时使用
适用于需要快速构建数据库驱动、功能丰富的 Web 应用，如内容管理系统、社交平台、电子商务网站。尤其适合团队协作开发，因其内置管理后台、ORM、认证系统等，可大幅减少重复工作。

## 示例
```Python
# 在 models.py 中定义模型
from django.db import models

class BlogPost(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    content = models.TextField()
    pub_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title

# 在 views.py 中定义视图
from django.shortcuts import render
from .models import BlogPost

def blog_list(request):
    posts = BlogPost.objects.all().order_by('-pub_date')
    return render(request, 'blog/list.html', {'posts': posts})

# 在 urls.py 中配置 URL
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('', views.blog_list, name='blog_list'),
]
```

## 反例
避免在视图中直接编写原始 SQL 查询，应使用 Django ORM 进行数据库操作，否则会丧失 ORM 的安全性和跨数据库兼容性。

## 边界条件
注意 Django 默认的时区设置（USE_TZ=True），处理时间相关字段时需确保数据库和代码时区一致；生产环境务必关闭 DEBUG=True 并配置静态文件服务。
common_pitfalls: 忘记运行 `python manage.py makemigrations` 和 `python manage.py migrate` 导致模型变更未同步到数据库，出现表不存在的错误。
在模板中直接使用 `{{ user.is_authenticated }}` 但未在视图上下文传递 `request`，导致模板变量未定义。
生产环境未设置 `ALLOWED_HOSTS` 导致 HTTP 400 错误，或未关闭 DEBUG 导致敏感信息泄露。
